items.title: How long is Anthropic’s lease with SpaceX? Opinions vary.
items.summary: Elon Musk is publicly reframing xAI’s massive Anthropic compute deal as short-term and cancellable, despite SpaceX’s own S-1 filing describing payments through May 2029.

items.title: Sesame, the conversational AI startup from Oculus founders, launches its iOS app
items.summary: Sesame’s new iOS app brings its conversational AI agents to the public, offering more natural back-and-forth interactions designed to feel less like traditional chatbots and more like talking to a person.

items.title: Slate Auto will start taking orders for its low-cost EV on June 24
items.summary: The Bezos-backed EV startup has yet to announce final pricing for its vehicle, which is supposed to start shipping by the end of this year.

items.title: Sneak peek at new Siri app reveals Apple’s plans to take on ChatGPT and more
items.summary: New renders offer a closer look at Apple’s planned AI overhaul for iOS 27, including a redesigned Siri experience powered and standalone Siri app.

items.title: Rivian under investigation over rear suspension failures on R1 models
items.summary: At least two owners have lost control of their vehicles after a critical suspension component broke. In both cases, the vehicles had been previously serviced.

items.title: RSI is the new AGI — and it’s just as hard to pin down
items.summary: A new crop of AI labs are focused on recursive self-improvement — but the goal is proving elusive.

items.title: At TechCrunch Disrupt 2026: Databricks’ co-founder on what kills enterprise AI deals
items.summary: Enterprise AI is entering a different phase now, one where enterprises are no longer evaluating whether AI is exciting. They are evaluating whether it is safe to deploy broadly.

items.title: YouTube adds new podcast features, including an AI recommendation tool and ‘Auto speed’
items.summary: The update signals YouTube's ongoing efforts to compete with other platforms for podcast audiences.

items.title: Startup Battlefield 200 application deadline extended to June 8 after overwhelming demand
items.summary: After overwhelming demand from founders around the world, TechCrunch has extended the Startup Battlefield 200 application deadline to June 8. Nominate a standout startup or apply yours today.

items.title: 2 days left: Lock in ticket savings of up to $410 to TechCrunch Disrupt 2026
items.summary: Savings of up to $410 on TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 tickets end tomorrow, May 29, 11:59 p.m. PT. Register now to save and join 10,000+ tech leaders on October 13-15 in San Francisco.

items.title: Visa invests in Replit to power agentic payments for developers

items.title: Has the hunt for AI compute uncovered the next Cerebras?

items.title: Oura unveils its Ring 5 with a thinner, lighter design starting at $399
items.summary: The ring is 40% smaller than its predecessor, and comes with more accurate sensing, enhanced battery life, and more.

items.title: Vertu wants CEOs to run companies from an AI foldable starting at $6,880
items.summary: Built on top of the open source Hermes project, Vertu's new foldable combines AI-agent workflows, enterprise integrations, and ultra-premium luxury finishes.

items.title: Google engineer charged with insider trading after making $1.2M on Polymarket
items.summary: According to the complaint, a Google engineer risked over $2.7 million on wagers related to Google's 2025 Year in Search campaign.

items.title: Bad cholesterol slashed 62% by single dose of gene-editing drug in small trial
items.summary: The interim Phase I trial data was only from 35 people, but results look good so far.

items.title: Forecasters predict below-average hurricane season, advise against complacency
items.summary: Forecasters say expected El Niño should temper hurricanes in Atlantic, urge preparedness.

items.title: Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 4 is out in October, and it’s coming to the Switch 2
items.summary: Activision has officially announced Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 4 and says the game will launch on October 23rd, 2026.
